01_initial_request.target_name: Insulin signaling network (specifically GSK3 and MARK2/3)
01_initial_request.input_data: 3T3-L1 adipocyte cells and mouse adipose tissue
01_initial_request.user_intent: To delineate insulin signal transduction in adipocytes and identify dysregulated signaling nodes contributing to insulin resistance
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Quantitative atlas of phosphosites and partial reversal of insulin resistance measured by 2-deoxyglucose uptake

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Insulin resistance is a key defect preceding type 2 diabetes, but how adipocyte signaling networks are dysregulated is unknown. [Gap] There is an urgent need for global unbiased analyses of signaling in insulin-resistant cells. [Decision] To achieve delineation of insulin signal transduction, chose to use 3T3-L1 Cell Culture to perform induction of five distinct insulin resistance models on adipocytes, expecting to obtain impaired 2-deoxyglucose uptake.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: All five models conferred impaired uptake of radiolabeled 2-deoxyglucose into adipocyte cells following acute insulin treatment, confirming insulin resistance.

references.citation: Fazakerley et al., 2018; Hoehn et al., 2009
references.relevance: Previous reports establishing these models induce insulin resistance in humans and animals.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Reversible protein phosphorylation is essential for mediating insulin action. [Gap] Global signaling changes in insulin resistance have not been systematically addressed in adipocytes. [Decision] To achieve quantitative atlas of phosphosites, chose to use EasyPhos method to perform phosphoproteomic sample preparation on cell lysates, expecting to obtain enriched phosphopeptides.

parameters.digestion: LysC and trypsin 1:100 enzyme-protein ratio, 37°C overnight
parameters.resuspension: 5 μl MS loading buffer (2% ACN, 0.3% TFA)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Quantified 39,846 phosphopeptides corresponding to 29,311 phosphorylation sites on 3791 proteins across all models.

references.relevance: Describes the EasyPhos platform for high-throughput phosphoproteomics.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mass spectrometry-based phosphoproteomics makes it possible to study insulin signaling on a global scale. [Gap] High sensitivity and throughput are required to analyze acute signaling responses. [Decision] To achieve data acquisition, chose to use Q Exactive HF to perform LC-MS/MS analysis on enriched phosphopeptides, expecting to obtain RAW MS data.

parameters.column: 50 cm fused silica, ReproSil Pur C18-AQ, 1.9 μm
parameters.gradient: 3-19% buffer B over 60 min, 19-41% buffer B over 30 min

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated high-quality MS data with average Pearson's correlation of 0.89 for phosphoproteome samples.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] RAW MS data requires processing to identify and quantify peptides. [Gap] Accurate identification of phosphosites is needed for downstream analysis. [Decision] To achieve peptide quantification, chose to use MaxQuant to perform RAW data processing on MS files, expecting to obtain quantified protein and phosphopeptide tables.

parameters.database: Mus musculus UniProt database (July 2017, December 2019, January 2021 releases)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Processed data revealed 7564 proteins and 39,846 phosphopeptides quantified across all models.

references.relevance: MaxQuant enables high peptide identification rates and proteome-wide quantification.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Statistical analysis is required to identify insulin-regulated phosphopeptides. [Gap] Significance thresholds must be defined to distinguish regulated sites. [Decision] To achieve identification of regulated sites, chose to use R / Limma to perform statistical testing on phosphopeptide abundance, expecting to obtain list of insulin-regulated phosphopeptides.

parameters.test: t-tests comparing CTRL BAS to CTRL INS

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Detected 1,951 insulin-regulated phosphopeptides in control cells that had altered abundance following insulin treatment.

references.relevance: Limma powers differential expression analyses for omics studies.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mechanistically, signaling changes could be caused by dysregulation of protein kinases. [Gap] Existing database annotations may not be context-relevant. [Decision] To achieve kinase activity profiling, chose to use KSEA to perform kinase substrate enrichment analysis on insulin response data, expecting to obtain kinase activation profiles.

parameters.cutoff: Kinases with fewer than 10 substrates excluded

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recapitulated known activation profiles (AKT, mTOR), but GSK3 was erroneously reported as activated by insulin in control cells.

references.relevance: Benchmarking substrate-based kinase activity inference using phosphoproteomic data.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Existing annotations failed to accurately assess GSK3 activity. [Gap] High-quality list of putative GSK3 substrates in adipocytes is needed. [Decision] To achieve context-specific substrate identification, chose to use LY2090314 (GSK3i) to perform acute inhibitor treatment on adipocytes, expecting to obtain GSK3-responsive phosphopeptides.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 790 phosphopeptides (on 395 proteins) were responsive to acute treatment with GSK3 inhibitor.

references.relevance: Source of the highly selective GSK3 inhibitor LY2090314.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Not all GSK3i-responsive sites are direct substrates. [Gap] Need to enrich for direct substrates using motif information. [Decision] To achieve direct substrate identification, chose to use Motif Analysis to perform filtering on GSK3i-responsive phosphopeptides, expecting to obtain high-confidence GSK3 substrate list.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 290 phosphopeptides corresponding to 274 phosphosites on 184 proteins identified as putative adipocyte-specific GSK3 substrates.

references.relevance: Describes GSK3 substrate motif requirements including priming phosphosite.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] A custom atlas of GSK3 substrates is now available. [Gap] GSK3 activity in insulin resistance models needs re-evaluation. [Decision] To achieve accurate GSK3 activity assessment, chose to use KSEA to perform analysis using custom GSK3 substrate list on IR phosphoproteome, expecting to obtain GSK3 activity status in IR models.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: GSK3 was inactivated in response to insulin in control cells, and this inactivation was attenuated across all insulin resistance models.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Impaired deactivation of GSK3 by insulin was observed in all IR models. [Gap] Whether targeting GSK3 can reverse insulin resistance is unknown. [Decision] To achieve functional validation, chose to use GSK3 Inhibitors (LY2090314, CHIR99021, AZD2858) to perform pharmacological inhibition in insulin-resistant cells, expecting to obtain restored insulin sensitivity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: GSK3 inhibition increased cell surface endogenous GLUT4 and HA-GLUT4-mRuby3 in control, TNF and DEX-treated cells.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GLUT4 translocation is a key functional output of insulin signaling. [Gap] Quantitative measure of glucose uptake is needed to confirm sensitivity reversal. [Decision] To achieve quantification of insulin sensitivity, chose to use 2DG Uptake Assay to perform glucose uptake measurement in treated cells and explants, expecting to obtain uptake rates.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Acute GSK3 inhibition partially restored insulin sensitivity in cells and tissue explants (e.g., increased uptake at 0.5 nM insulin in HFD explants).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] MARK2/3 phosphosites were defective across multiple models. [Gap] Functional role of MARK2/3 in insulin action needs validation. [Decision] To achieve validation of MARK2/3 role, chose to use siRNA Transfection to perform knockdown of Mark2/3 in adipocytes, expecting to obtain changes in GLUT4 translocation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: siRNA-mediated knockdown of Mark2/3 increased insulin-stimulated GLUT4 translocation.

references.relevance: Describes siRNA-based protein knockdown in 3T3-L1 adipocytes.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Key findings from 3T3-L1 cells need validation in vivo. [Gap] Whether GSK3 dysregulation occurs in insulin-resistant adipose tissue is unknown. [Decision] To achieve in vivo validation, chose to use Mouse HFD Model to perform phosphoproteomic analysis of epididymal adipose tissue, expecting to obtain signaling profiles in CHOW, HFD, and REV mice.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Insulin-stimulated GSK3 deactivation was impaired in HFD and partially restored in REV tissue, recapitulating 3T3-L1 findings.

references.relevance: Describes diet-induced insulin resistance models in mice.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Phosphoproteomics, 2-Deoxyglucose Uptake Assay, GLUT4 Translocation Assay, Western Blotting

phosphopeptides_quantified.interpretation: Comprehensive atlas of adipocyte phosphoproteome established.

insulin_regulated_sites.interpretation: Defined insulin-responsive signaling network in control cells.

glut4_translocation_increase.interpretation: GSK3 inhibition and MARK2/3 knockdown increased insulin-stimulated GLUT4 translocation.

2dg_uptake_restoration.interpretation: Acute GSK3 inhibition partially reversed insulin resistance in cells and tissue explants.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. A quantitative atlas of phosphosites was generated, dysregulated signaling nodes (GSK3, MARK2/3) were identified, and partial reversal of insulin resistance was demonstrated via 2-deoxyglucose uptake assays following pharmacological inhibition.
